core_model.structure_summary: Minimization of a free-energy functional that bounds the log-partition function of a factorized posterior, built from Kullback-Leibler divergences between a factorized trial distribution and a prior plus a quadratic data-fidelity coupling through a linear operator, whose stationary points coincide with a message-passing fixed point.
core_model.canonical_form: \min_{\{a_i\},\{c_i\}} \mathcal F = \sum_i D_{\mathrm{KL}}(Q_i\Vert P_0) + \frac{1}{2\Delta}\sum_\mu\Bigl[(y_\mu - \textstyle\sum_i F_{\mu i}a_i)^2 + \sum_i F_{\mu i}^2 c_i\Bigr], \quad y = Fx + \xi

variables.meaning: per-coordinate means and variances of a factorized trial distribution being estimated
free-energy functional (KL divergence terms plus quadratic residual) to be minimized
fixed linear operator (measurement matrix) coupling latent coordinates to observations
additive Gaussian perturbation on the linear observations

notes: Underlying object is a linear observation model y=Fx+noise inferred via variational free-energy minimization; the paper's results are about the free energies (naive mean-field and Bethe) and their stationary points, so I take 'variational' as the core object rather than the generative 'statistical_model'. Equation extraction is noisy but the free-energy structure is clear.
